banana.fact: Bananas contain more tryptophan than most foods — this is why they help your body ease into sleep.

spinach.fact: Your body absorbs the iron in spinach up to 6x better when you pair it with vitamin C — add a strawberry or a squeeze of lemon.

blueberries.fact: Blueberries hold one of the highest antioxidant concentrations of any fruit — a single cup has more than a whole apple.

oats.fact: The beta-glucan fiber in oats forms a gel in your gut that slows sugar absorption — this is why oat smoothies keep you full for hours.

chia-seed.fact: Chia seeds absorb up to 12x their weight in liquid — this is what turns your smoothie thick and keeps you hydrated longer.

chia-seeds.fact: Chia seeds absorb up to 12x their weight in liquid — this is what turns your smoothie thick and keeps you hydrated longer.

flaxseed.fact: Flaxseeds only release their omega-3s and fiber benefits when ground — whole flaxseeds pass through you almost unchanged.

kale.fact: Kale has more vitamin C per gram than an orange — 100g of kale delivers about 120% of your daily target.

ginger.fact: Ginger contains gingerol, a compound that works on the same nausea pathway as motion-sickness medication — without the drowsiness.

avocado.fact: Avocado has more potassium per serving than a banana — one half provides about 15% of your daily need.

apple.fact: Most of the fiber and antioxidants in an apple live in the skin — peeling it strips about 30% of its nutritional value.

strawberry.fact: Strawberries contain more vitamin C than oranges — one cup delivers about 150% of your daily target.

greek-yogurt.fact: Greek yogurt has nearly double the protein of regular yogurt because the whey is strained out during production.

peanut-butter.fact: Peanut butter has about 4g of plant-based protein per tablespoon — but only if you pick a jar where peanuts are the only ingredient.
peanut-butter.source_hint: Label check — avoid added sugars & oils

almond-milk.fact: Unsweetened almond milk has about 30 calories per cup — about 1/4 the calories of dairy milk, but also about 1/8 the protein.

oat-milk.fact: Oat milk is often the creamiest plant milk in a smoothie — its natural beta-glucan fiber gives it a thicker mouthfeel than almond or rice milk.

turmeric.fact: Turmeric's active compound curcumin absorbs up to 20x better when paired with black pepper — a tiny pinch changes everything.

mango.fact: A single mango delivers more than 100% of your daily vitamin C — and about 35% of your daily vitamin A.

pineapple.fact: Pineapple is the only common fruit that contains bromelain — an enzyme that actually helps digest protein in your stomach.

cinnamon.fact: Cinnamon can help your body respond better to insulin — even a half teaspoon a day has measurable effects in studies.

_default.fact: Every ingredient you blend affects your smoothie differently based on your goal — check the full analysis to see how this one fits yours.
